Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 222. Pulling up Apple Tree Saplings (ABL 0938)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-a

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Samnuhu-beli-uṣur.

o 22

ARAD-ka mdsa-am-nu-ḫu—U—PAB

o 33

DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-a

(3) Good health to the king, my lord!

o 44

ina UGU GIŠ.ziq-⸢pi* ša⸣ LUGAL

(4) As to the saplings about which the king, my lord, wrote to me, I have pulled up 1,000 apple-tree saplings from where [there were] 3 or [......].

o 55

be-lí iš-pur-an-ni

o 66

01 lim GIŠ.ziq-pu ⸢ša*⸣ GIŠ.ḪAŠḪUR*-MEŠ

o 77

É 03 É [x x x]-⸢ni*⸣

o 88

a-ta-sa-⸢ḫa⸣ [x x x x x]

o 99

⸢LÚv*.qur*⸣-[bu-ti x x x x]

(9) A royal bo[dyguard ......

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr uninscribed

(SPACER)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334634/.
